issue: 前端实现收敛为唯一版本

app.qrc 精简为只打包必须资源（graph_spider实现 + redirect壳）

所有历史实现已归档到 _archive/
QRC 只包含实际运行必须的资源

description: GUI改为全黑主题、优化边显示逻辑、调整面板大小
changes: spider.css: 所有背景改为纯黑(#000000)，移除渐变
spider.css: 所有UI元素(input/select/panel/results/toast)使用黑色背景
spider.js: canvas背景从#0b0f14改为#000000
spider.js: 添加getPathToRoot函数追踪从根到选中节点的路径
spider.js: 修改边绘制逻辑，选中节点时只显示路径上的边
spider.js: 路径边使用更亮的青色(.88)和更粗线宽(2.5)
MainWindow.cpp: 文件树宽度限制180-280px，初始220px
MainWindow.cpp: splitter初始比例220:980
MainWindow.cpp: Details dock最小宽320px，初始380px
rationale: 黑色主题减少眼睛疲劳，路径高亮大幅减少视觉混乱，合理的面板大小提升可用性

description: 应用sddai_fix_details_popout.patch修复Details窗口浮动显示问题
changes: 新增 DetailsWindow 类（独立顶层窗口，避免re-parent问题）
detailsDock 禁用 DockWidgetFloatable（避免浮动触发blank）
添加 View -> Open Details Window 菜单项
MainWindow::openDetailsWindow() 创建并显示独立Details窗口
添加 qt_style.qss 深色主题样式（菜单栏、滚动条、TreeView）
main.cpp 启动时加载 :/qt_style.qss 应用全局样式
detailsDock 添加 visibilityChanged 防御性reload机制
DetailsWindow::reloadPage() 强制重新加载避免偶发不刷新
rationale: QWebEngineView在QDockWidget浮动时会因re-parent到新顶层窗口导致渲染失败，独立窗口从始至终都是top-level，100%稳定
verification: Dock不再允许浮动（Features去掉Floatable）
View菜单有Open Details Window选项
点击后弹出独立Details窗口并正确显示内容
Qt原生滚动条样式美观（深色+圆角）

issue: API-authored generated project source fails runnable MVP validation
description: Live phone-to-PC voice-assistant generation used api_agent and provider-authored source files, but blocked at source_generation because generated files imported uninstalled Flask dependencies, had mismatched public interfaces, incomplete README sections, and missing UI/export evidence.
changes: Strengthen source-generation prompt so validation paths must run in the current verifier environment without pip/poetry/npm install
Require standard-library HTTP/web implementation or generated local fallback instead of Flask/FastAPI/Django/requests-style undeclared dependencies
Require --serve and rich export commands to exit 0 under verifier probes instead of blocking forever
Include dependency, README quality, and UX blockers in previous-failure retry feedback
rationale: The failure is recurring generated-source quality drift, not local template fallback; API retries need exact runnable-project constraints and blocker evidence.
verification: Focused source-generation prompt tests assert dependency-free runnable guidance and previous-failure feedback

issue: API-authored generated project repeats cross-file integration defects after runnable-source prompt hardening
description: A fresh phone-to-PC voice-assistant run no longer failed on Flask, but the generated project still failed as a concrete user project because src-layout modules used bare sibling imports, package re-exports drifted, a service constructor called CommandWhitelist without required arguments, and the web/mobile endpoints never became reachable.
changes: Add an explicit Builder/Integration QA/Product QA/Delivery QA handoff protocol to the source-generation prompt
Require src-layout package modules to avoid bare sibling imports and require entrypoint scripts to import concrete existing package symbols
Require an API signature matrix covering model constructors, service methods, route handlers, exporter functions, and tests
Translate previous runtime probe errors into actionable import, re-export, constructor, and local server repair hints for the next API retry
rationale: The defect class is agent interaction failure: validator evidence existed, but the next builder prompt did not make the QA handoff concrete enough to prevent repeated incompatible files.
verification: Focused source-generation prompt tests assert live failure classes are carried into retry instructions

issue: API-authored generated project can fail after syntax/import checks because generated tests use src.<package> imports and runtime calls mismatch service signatures
description: The live phone-to-PC voice assistant run reached API source_generation without local fallback, but the generated bundle still failed on VoiceAssistantService constructor arguments and tests importing src.readme. This is a recurring generated-source self-check gap, not a project-specific template problem.
changes: Add generic generated-test validation under generic_validation
Reject generated tests that import src.<package> instead of the package directly with generated src on PYTHONPATH
Run generated unittest discovery as validation evidence and store stdout/stderr tails
Feed generated-test failures and runtime signature errors into the next source_generation retry prompt
rationale: Provider-authored source should repair itself from validation evidence. CTCP should supply generic self-check and retry feedback rather than local deterministic project templates or post-generation source patching.
verification: tests.test_generated_project_validation_self_repair covers src.<package> generated-test rejection and retry-prompt consumption of generated-test/signature failures

issue: API-authored generated project still repeats cross-file constructor and dataclass signature drift after generated-test self-repair validation
description: Concrete speed test run voice-assistant-speed-20260508 used api_agent for all critical steps with fallback_count=0 and reached 10 source_generation attempts, but the generated phone-to-PC voice assistant remained blocked at generic_validation because startup/export called VoiceAssistantService without the required whitelist and generated tests called CommandWhitelist(commands=...) although that constructor did not accept commands.
changes: Recorded speed-test evidence instead of patching generated project source
Confirmed generated-test validation captures the remaining signature drift
Identified next repair target as stronger provider-side cross-file interface planning/validation before source_generation returns
rationale: The repeated defect is no longer template fallback or missing generated-test feedback; API source_generation needs stricter cross-file interface contract synthesis so service, app, seed, and tests agree on constructors and return types before the JSON bundle is accepted.
verification: voice-assistant-speed-20260508: new-run 0.603s, advance 1278.028s, fallback_count=0, all_critical_steps_api=true, source_generation_attempts=10, generic_validation.passed=false

issue: API-authored generated project cross-file signature drift must be caught before delivery
description: The source_generation generic validator now performs conservative AST-based checks across generated Python files to compare generated class/function definitions with generated direct call sites. It reports missing required arguments, unexpected keywords, and too many positional arguments as python_signature_consistency evidence, and source_generation retries now consume those exact caller/callee details.
changes: Added tools/providers/project_generation_signature_validation.py
Integrated python_signature_consistency into generic_validation pass/fail and source_generation_report evidence
Added retry-prompt rendering for signature_consistency mismatches
Added regression coverage for VoiceAssistantService missing whitelist and CommandWhitelist unexpected commands keyword
rationale: CTCP should force provider-authored source to repair cross-file interface drift from validation evidence instead of relying on local templates or manual post-generation fixes.
verification: .venv\\Scripts\\python.exe -m unittest tests.test_generated_project_signature_validation -v: PASS, 3 tests OK
.venv\\Scripts\\python.exe -m unittest tests.test_generated_project_validation_self_repair -v: PASS, 2 tests OK
.venv\\Scripts\\python.exe -m unittest discover -s tests -p "test_project_generation_artifacts.py" -v: PASS, 48 tests OK

issue: Signature validation catches generated-source drift but API source_generation still does not converge to a deliverable project
description: Live retest run voice-assistant-signature-retest-20260508 used gpt-4.1 through api_agent and generated 19 files, but remained blocked at generic_validation. The new python_signature_consistency gate reported concrete mismatches including VoiceAssistantService(command_whitelist=...) vs VoiceAssistantService(whitelist=...), run_server(host=...) unexpected keyword, and CommandRequest dataclass calls using command_text/input_mode instead of command,args. Generated tests also called an abstract service_contract method that raises NotImplementedError.
changes: Recorded live retest evidence without manually repairing generated source
Confirmed python_signature_consistency is connected and catches the failure class
Stopped after a bounded retry timed out to avoid unbounded API spend
rationale: The validator now exposes the problem, but source_generation still lacks enough interface-planning/self-repair discipline to make API-authored files converge after feedback. The next repair should target source_generation batch planning/abstract-interface implementation alignment, not local project templates.
verification: voice-assistant-signature-retest-20260508: new-run 0.699s, advance 286.579s, retry timed out after 604.8s, generated_files=19, missing_required=0, generic_validation.passed=false, python_signature_consistency.passed=false, generated_tests.passed=false, smoke_run.passed=false

issue: Source-generation retry needs structured signature-matrix and abstract-stub blockers
description: The generated-project signature validator now rejects provider-declared signature matrices that disagree with actual generated Python definitions and blocks generated runtime files that contain methods raising NotImplementedError. The source_generation prompt now requires interfaces[path].signatures and feeds signature_matrix plus abstract_stub failures back into retries.
changes: Added interface_signature_mismatches to python_signature_consistency validation
Added abstract_stub_violations for generated runtime Python files that raise NotImplementedError
Passed provider interface_contract into signature validation
Strengthened source_generation prompt with interfaces[path].signatures and abstract-stub repair requirements
Added focused regression tests for signature matrix drift and NotImplementedError stubs
rationale: The previous validator exposed call-site drift, but API batches could still contradict their own declared interfaces or route runtime/test paths through abstract stubs. Structured blockers make the next retry repair more concrete without adding local templates.
verification: .venv\\Scripts\\python.exe -m unittest tests.test_generated_project_signature_validation -v: PASS, 5 tests OK
.venv\\Scripts\\python.exe -m unittest tests.test_generated_project_validation_self_repair -v: PASS, 2 tests OK
.venv\\Scripts\\python.exe -m unittest discover -s tests -p "test_project_generation_artifacts.py" -v: PASS, 48 tests OK

issue: Fresh API generation still fails before delivery because intent/spec collapse and stale prompt constraints leak into source_generation
description: Live API retest voice-assistant-interface-repair-api-20260509 used gpt-4.1 through api_agent with fallback_count=0 and 7 OK source_generation API calls, but advance timed out before writing source_generation_report.json. The partially materialized project collapsed to project_output/readme with package readme and generic_software_project intent, while the source_generation prompt still contained stale VN/GUI requirements such as story/scene/branch editor and run_project_gui compatibility. Generated source parsed, but --serve was not implemented, --headless failed on dict-vs-string command samples, and generated unittest discovery failed with service/test API drift.
changes: Recorded API retest evidence without manually repairing generated source
Stopped two residual advance processes after the 20 minute timeout to avoid duplicate API spend
Confirmed critical plan/review/output_contract steps used api_agent and no local fallback
Confirmed generated project self-check failures with py_compile/help/headless/--serve/unittest commands
rationale: The next repair should address upstream intent/spec shaping and stale domain-specific prompt leakage before source_generation, not add local templates or manually patch generated output. Stronger signature validation alone cannot converge when the project contract itself is generic/stale.
verification: new-run rc=0 in 0.602s
advance timed out after 1204.604s with run blocked waiting for artifacts/source_generation_report.json
provider_ledger_summary: critical_step_count=7, critical_api_step_count=7, fallback_count=0, all_critical_steps_api=true
api_calls.jsonl: source_generation count=7, model=gpt-4.1, status=OK
generated project: py_compile PASS; --help PASS; --serve FAIL unrecognized arguments; --headless FAIL AttributeError dict has no strip; unittest FAIL 1 failure and 3 errors
